questions.question: Which global alliance announced collaboration on ‘Hypersonic missile strike and defence capacity’?
questions.explanation: The United States, Britain and Australia announced that they would begin collaborating on hypersonic missile strike and defence capacity. This is in response to their rivals Russia and China advancing rapidly in the hypersonic technology. The countries would work on hypersonics in an expansion of their AUKUS defence alliance. This will equip Australia with nuclear-powered submarines to counter China’s growing military activities.

questions.question: International Monetary Fund (IMF) has lauded which Indian scheme for preventing extreme poverty?
questions.explanation: In a recent report, the International Monetary Fund (IMF) has appreciated the Pradhan Mantri Garib Kalyan Yojana. It highlighted that the program provided insurance to the poor and prevented an increase in the prevalence of extreme poverty in the country. Without any food subsidies, extreme poverty in the pandemic would have increased by 1.05 per cent.

questions.question: Which Union Ministry is associated with the Weapons of Mass Destruction and their Delivery Systems (Prohibition of Unlawful Activities) Act?
questions.explanation: ‘Weapons of Mass Destruction and their Delivery Systems (Prohibition of Unlawful Activities) Amendment Bill, 2022’ was introduced by the External Affairs Minister in Lok Sabha. The Bill, which was passed by the Lok Sabha amends the original act of 2005. The bill seeks to prohibit financing of any activity in relation to weapons of mass destruction and their delivery systems and empowers the Central Government to freeze, seize or attach funds for preventing such financing.

questions.question: Which institution recently launched the ‘AVSAR’ scheme?
questions.explanation: The Airports Authority of India (AAI) has recently launched Airport as Venue for skilled artisans Of The Region (AVSAR) scheme. It aims to allocate space to Self Help Groups (SHGs) at its airports for selling or showcasing the self-made products of their region. Under this scheme, an area of 100-200 square feet has been earmarked at each AAI-operated airport.

questions.question: Which Union Ministry is associated with the Citizenship (Amendment) Act, 2019?

options.label: D
options.text: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs

questions.explanation: The Ministry of Home Affairs (MHA) has sought another six-month extension to frame the rules of the Citizenship (Amendment) Act, 2019 (CAA). The amendment seeks to fast-track the citizenship process of six non-Muslim undocumented communities from Pakistan, Bangladesh and Afghanistan. This is also the fifth extension sought by the Union Home Ministry.

questions.question: Which country supplies ‘MH-60 ‘Romeo’ multi-role helicopters’ to India?
questions.explanation: India is procuring 24 MH-60 Romeo Multi-role helicopters as part of an Rs 15,000-crore deal with the US Government. The first batch of the aircrew of the Indian Navy has completed its training in the US to operate the MH-60 ‘Romeo’ multi-role helicopters. MH-60R helicopter, manufactured by Lockheed Martin Corporation, is an all-weather helicopter designed to support multiple mission.

questions.question: What percent of the domestic capital procurement budget is earmarked for private industries?
questions.explanation: The Defence Ministry announced that 25% of the domestic capital procurement budget, amounting to more than Rs. 21,000 crore will be earmarked for private industries in the financial year of 2022-23. Rs. 1500 crore will be earmarked for procurement from start-ups. Ministry of Defence had earlier decided to earmark 68% of the capital procurement budget for the entire domestic industry during the financial year of 2022-23.

questions.question: ‘Nadabet Seema Darshan project’ was inaugurated in which Indian state?
questions.explanation: As part of the Seema Darshan project, Union Home Minister Amit Shah inaugurated an Indo-Pakistan border viewing point in Nadabet in Gujarat. Located in the Rann of Kutch region, this place is also known as the ‘Wagah of Gujarat’. Civilians get access to view the fenced international border with Pakistan at ‘Zero Point’, which is guarded round the clock by the Border Security Force (BSF).

questions.question: Who is the first recipient of the ‘Lata Deenanath Mangeshkar Award’?
questions.explanation: Prime Minister Narendra Modi is set to be preented with the first Lata Deenanath Mangeshkar Award at the 80th annual Master Deenanath Mangeshkar Awards ceremony. The award was instituted in the memory and honour of singer Lata Mangeshkar who passed away at the age of 92 years. The award will be given every year to an individual who has made exemplary contributions to our nation, its people and our society.

questions.question: India signed a new ‘Space Situational Awareness arrangement’ with which country?
questions.explanation: India and the USA finalized signing of a new Space Situational Awareness arrangement, during the 2+2 Ministerial Dialogue being held at Washington. The United States and India finalized major bilateral initiatives on information-sharing, liaison exchanges, and joint service engagements to support high-end, combined operations.
